title: Where clinical expertise meets human connection.
seo_title: About Wildwood Psychotherapy in Leesburg, VA

blocks.body:
You’ve built a life that looks enviable from the outside, but the internal cost of maintaining that success has become unsustainable. I work with the "reliable" ones, the people who've spent decades perfecting the art of "fine" while their own well-being sits on the back burner. You aren't just looking for a listener who will tell you everything will be okay. You're looking for someone who understands that it won't be okay until you actually deal with what's underneath.

I get it. Not because I read it in a textbook, but because I've sat across from countless adults and couples who are exactly where you are right now. Successful on paper. Struggling underneath. Wondering if this is just how life is supposed to feel.

Spoiler: **It's not.**

blocks.title: Where Insight Meets Integration
blocks.subtitle: The Wildwood Methodology
blocks.body:
Most therapy focuses on managing symptoms. You learn coping skills, talk about your week, and maybe feel a little better for a few days.

### Beyond Symptom Management

But here's what I've learned after years of working with developmental trauma: If we're only addressing surface symptoms, we're just rearranging furniture in a burning house.

The anxiety you feel in your relationship? The overworking that you can't seem to stop? The drinking that's crept up over the years? Those aren't the problems. They're solutions you created to survive situations that were too much to handle at the time.

My job isn't to take away your coping mechanisms. It's to help you understand why you need them in the first place, and then give you better options.

I'm EMDR Certified, which means I've completed advanced training beyond basic certification. I blend EMDR with Internal Family Systems work, mindfulness, DBT, and ACT because trauma doesn't live in just one part of your brain or body. It's everywhere. And healing it requires more than one approach.

**But here's what matters more than my credentials: I won't waste your time or money keeping you comfortable. If you want someone who just nods sympathetically while you talk, I'm not your person. If you want someone who will challenge you, celebrate your progress, and help you actually change the patterns that are keeping you stuck, let's talk.**

blocks.title: Who I Work With Best
blocks.subtitle: The Clients I Love Working With
blocks.body:
I'm most energized when working with individuals and couples who are done with performing and ready for presence. My practice is designed for the people who have spent a lifetime being the reliable one, only to realize they’ve become a stranger to themselves.

I specialize in working with adults (typically ages 30 to 70+) who are high-functioning on the outside but navigating a complex internal landscape. You don’t need a therapist to tell you, "It’s going to be okay."

**You need a guide who can help you navigate the nuances of:**

* Struggling with anxiety or depression that seems to come out of nowhere
* Noticing old relationship patterns showing up in new relationships
* Using work, alcohol, or avoidance to cope with feelings you can't name
* Feeling disconnected from yourself and unsure who you are outside your roles
* Dealing with attachment wounds that make intimacy feel terrifying and necessary at the same time

My work with couples extends into the nuanced landscapes of ethical non-monogamy and polyamory. In this space, your relationship structure is respected as a fundamental starting point, not an oddity to be questioned. We move directly into the sophisticated work of navigating poly-secure attachment, complex boundary negotiation, and the unique communication hurdles that arise in non-traditional dynamics. While every relationship's internal landscape is unique, you'll find a partner here who is already fluent in the language of the lifestyle. We can focus on the work of deep connection rather than basic education.

blocks.title: My Approach
blocks.subtitle: Depth, Directness, and Grounded Insight
blocks.body:
I'm warm and direct. I'll meet you where you are and hold space for whatever shows up in our sessions.

Some sessions will feel heavy. Some will feel lighter. All of them will move you forward if you're willing to do the work, because I'm tracking patterns you might not even notice yet. I'm paying attention to what you're saying and what you're not saying. I'm watching for the moments when your nervous system takes over, and your rational brain checks out.

And I'm going to point those moments out because that's where the real work happens. But whether you decide to explore them, sit with them, or work through them? That's your choice. This is your healing. I'm here to hold space for it.

### Science-Backed & Human-Centered

I lead with EMDR and IFS-informed parts work because they go where conversation cannot - reaching the neurological roots of your story to facilitate true unburdening.

EMDR helps your brain reprocess traumatic memories so they stop hijacking your present. Parts work helps you understand the different aspects of yourself that are often in conflict. A blend of mindfulness, DBT, and ACT provides practical tools to regulate your emotions and build a life that actually aligns with your values.

But none of those techniques matter if we don't have a solid therapeutic relationship. **Healing happens in connection.** That means our relationship matters just as much as any intervention I use.

I'm committed to ongoing training and staying current with trauma research because your healing deserves a therapist who never stops learning.

blocks.title: Behind the Practice: A Little More About Me
blocks.subtitle: Outside the Therapy Room
blocks.body:
I live in Western Loudoun with my family. Outside of sessions, I'm probably reading the latest trauma research, messing with my plants, or wrangling kids, a dog, and a seemingly endless flow of (at times) wild ideas.

I believe in practicing what I preach, which means I'm also doing my own work. I can't ask you to be vulnerable if I'm not willing to do the same.

I didn't become a therapist because I had all the answers. I became a therapist because I'm deeply curious about what makes humans *human* the way they do, and I'm committed to helping people untangle the knots that keep them stuck in patterns they desperately want to break.

items.title: Education & Credentials
items.content:
- EMDRIA Certified Therapist™
- Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC) - Virginia #0701015737
- Master of Education in Clinical Mental Health Counseling - The College of William & Mary
- Bachelor of Arts in Communication - Virginia Polytechnic Institute & State University

items.title: Specialized Training
items.content:
- EMDR Therapy (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing)
- IFS (Internal Family Systems)-Informed Parts Work
- Non-monogamous & Polyamorous Relationships
- Attachment-Based Therapy for Developmental Trauma
- Neurodiversity-Affirming Care
- Gottman Level 1 Trained for Couples Work
- One-year clinical internship at Trauma Specialists of Maryland

items.title: Professional Memberships
items.content:
- American Counseling Association (ACA)
- National Board for Certified Counselors (NBCC)
- EMDR International Association (EMDRIA)
- Counselors for Social Justice (CSJ)
- Society for Sexual, Affectional, Intersex, & Gender Expansive Identities (SAIGE)
- Association of Counseling Sexology & Sexual Wellness (ACSSW)
- Virginia Mental Health Counselors Association (VAMHCA)

subtitle: I’m Kaylie Groenhout, LPC and EMDR Certified Therapist in Leesburg helping high-achieving adults trade performance for presence.
